directive.title: Error handling and logging infrastructure setup

auditor.summary: The generated code provides a solid foundation for the error handling system with proper TypeScript types, RFC 7807 API error responses, and structured logging. However, the build is incomplete due to truncation (recovered_from_truncation: true), missing critical components like ErrorBoundary, client-reporter, and the full audit trail implementation. Several security and reliability issues need addressing: the audit hash uses a simple hash instead of SHA-256, the request context storage is not thread-safe, and the AUDIT_SECRET handling is insecure. The build is CONDITIONAL with a score of 72, meaning it can ship with the noted fixes, but the missing components must be completed before production deployment.

top_issues.description: The audit hash uses a synchronous simple hash (createSimpleHash) instead of the SHA-256 hash specified in the build spec. The async createHash function is defined but never used. This violates the EQS §4.1 requirement for SHA-256 based audit hashing.

The audit chain implementation uses an in-memory array (auditChain) that is not persisted and will be lost on server restart. This violates the immutable logging requirement. Additionally, the chain hash computation is a simple hash, not SHA-256.

The request context storage uses globalThis.requestContext which is not thread-safe and will cause race conditions in concurrent request handling. The AsyncLocalStorage-based approach specified in the build spec is not implemented.
